== Notes for [[Item:Q52548|Q52548]] ==

{geni:about_me} HE RULED OVER KOSALA KINGDOM WITH AYODHYA AS ITS CAPITAL. THE FIRST PROMINENT MONARCH OF THIS DYNASTY, GIVING THE DYNASTY ITS ANOTHER NAME THE IKSHVAKU DYNASTY. IKSHAVAKU WAS THE FIRST KING OF THE IKSHAVAKU DYNASTY AND FOUNDER OF THE SOLAR DYNASTY OF KAHATRIYAS IN VEDIC CIVILIZATION IN ANCIENT INDIA. IKSHAVAKU WAS THE FIRST KING TO IMPLEMENT THE MANUSMRITIOR THE FIVE RELIGIOUS RULES OF HINDU LIVING COMPOSED THROUGH DIVINE INSPIRATION AND FROM THE VEDAS BY HIS FATHER MANU VALVATSA. HE IS REMEMBERED IN HINDU SCRIPTURES AS A RIGHTEOUS AND GLORIOUS KING.
<p>--------------------</p>
<p>Manu is a title accorded to the progenitor of mankind, and also the very first king to rule this earth, who saved mankind from the universal flood. The word Man surely originates from Manu or "Manav" in Sanskrit.</p>
<p>http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manu_(Hinduism)</p>
<p>--------------------</p>
<p>THE FIRST PROMINENT MONARCH OF THIS DYNASTY, GIVING THE DYNASTY ITS ANOTHER NAME THE IKSHVAKU DYNASTY.</p>
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/index.html</p>
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/ikshvaku.html</p>
<p>--------------------</p>
<p>Ikshvaku (Sanskrit: इक्ष्वाकु ‘sugar cane’[1], pāli: Okkāka) was the first king of the Ikshvaku dynasty and founder of the Solar Dynasty of Kshatriyas in Vedic civilization in ancient India.</p>
<p>http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ikshvaku</p>

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
